title: Tender Greek Butter Beans Stew
description: A hearty, herbal, and indulgent Greek stew featuring tender butter beans, perfect for a comforting meal. This vegetarian-friendly dish is made with a pressure cooker for ease and speed.
instructions: Sort and rinse the dried butter beans, then soak them in water for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the soaked butter beans, then set them aside.
Heat the olive oil in the pressure cooker over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it's translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the chopped parsley, dill, lemon juice, tomato paste, vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper. Stir well to combine.
Add the soaked and drained butter beans to the pressure cooker, making sure they're covered with the vegetable broth.
Close the lid of the pressure cooker and set the valve to 'sealing'. Cook on high pressure for 20 minutes, then let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes.
Open the lid and check if the butter beans are tender. If not, close the lid and cook for another 5-10 minutes or until they're tender.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional parsley and lemon wedges if desired.
